title: Up All Night

jokes.text: They're very expensive freshwater clams from the... Cuyahoga River.
jokes.setup: Jack defends expensive seafood gift that caused food poisoning
jokes.punchline: The Cuyahoga River is famously polluted, making 'expensive freshwater clams' absurd
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character moment - his obliviousness to obvious problems while maintaining corporate speak. Cuyahoga River reference is specific and earned.

jokes.text: My whole staff has been blorching for three days
jokes.setup: Describing food poisoning symptoms
jokes.punchline: 'Blorching' - a perfectly disgusting made-up word for vomiting
jokes.explanation: Solid made-up word that sounds appropriately gross. Economy is excellent - one word does all the work.

jokes.text: You make me want to vomit!
jokes.setup: Following discussion of food poisoning and blorching
jokes.punchline: Tracy's dramatic declaration connects to the vomiting theme
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that maintains the vomit theme while being character-appropriate for Tracy's drama.

jokes.text: one minute, you're newlyweds making love on the floor of the Concorde. Then the next, your lawyers are fighting over who gets to keep the box your dog defecates in.
jokes.setup: Jack describing the arc of his marriage from romantic to petty
jokes.punchline: Escalates from glamorous Concorde sex to fighting over a dog's litter box
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure and perfect Jack character voice. The specific absurdity of the dog box is earned and memorable.

jokes.text: You taught your dog to poop in a box? Bianca did. But I want that box.
jokes.setup: Liz's reasonable question about the dog box
jokes.punchline: Jack's petty determination to claim ownership of a poop box
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up that shows Jack's competitive pettiness. The economy is excellent - maximum absurdity in minimal words.

jokes.text: Are you angry or excited right now? I can't tell.
jokes.setup: Jack's passionate description of dealing with his ex-wife
jokes.punchline: Liz pointing out Jack's unclear emotional state
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor that highlights Jack's complex relationship with conflict. Liz as straight-man works well.

jokes.text: This would work on 'Ugly Betty.'
jokes.setup: Someone commenting on a situation or visual
jokes.punchline: Meta reference to another NBC show, suggesting something is too soap opera-ish for 30 Rock
jokes.explanation: Typical 30 Rock meta humor but not deeply developed. Quick throwaway that works for the show's style.

jokes.text: Me and Angie rented the penthouse at the Soho Grand, where we will drink wine and pleasure each other.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining his Valentine's Day plans
jokes.punchline: Overly clinical/formal phrasing 'pleasure each other' for romantic plans
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy character moment - his weird formal way of describing intimate things. The clinical phrasing creates the humor.

jokes.text: When you've been married 17 years, you have to keep it spicy. That's why me and my wife role-play. She put on a girl scout outfit with a box of cookies, and I answer the door in my boxers.
jokes.setup: Explaining how to keep marriage exciting
jokes.punchline: Specific, uncomfortable roleplay scenario that's more creepy than romantic
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor through TMI oversharing. The specificity makes it funnier and more uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Or I rent out a wheelchair, and she pretends to be my caseworker. And, in a way, she is.
jokes.setup: Continuing the roleplay theme
jokes.punchline: Dark turn suggesting his wife actually is like a caseworker in their marriage
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark turn that reveals character dysfunction. The final 'and, in a way, she is' is perfect economy.

jokes.text: I just took a whole bunch of Cialis, because I have big Valentine's plans tonight. With who? Uh, nobody.
jokes.setup: Bragging about taking Cialis for big Valentine's plans
jokes.punchline: Admits he has no actual date
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline structure with good awkward reveal. The timing and structure work well.

jokes.text: he's still insisting on having a Greek Orthodox wedding. But I really disagree with the church's stance on Cyprus.
jokes.setup: Young woman having relationship problems
jokes.punchline: Her objection is to complex geopolitical issues rather than typical wedding concerns
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Cerie's unexpected depth and specific knowledge. Subverts expectations about young attractive characters.

jokes.text: I want all of your parents' love letters.
jokes.setup: Divorce demands escalating from personal items
jokes.punchline: Demanding even the parents' love letters shows extreme pettiness
jokes.explanation: Good escalation in the divorce demands list. Shows character pettiness effectively through specific absurdity.

jokes.text: Oh, damn it, Johnny! You know I love my Big Beef and Cheddar!
jokes.setup: Wealthy woman agreeing to give up various luxury items
jokes.punchline: She draws the line at losing access to Arby's fast food
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing unexpected priorities. The specific Arby's menu item makes it more absurd and quotable.

jokes.text: Because it's Valentine's Day, and you know I don't... Oh, no. It's what? It's Valentine's Day. Again?
jokes.setup: Liz assuming Frank would be nice on Valentine's Day
jokes.punchline: Frank's horror at discovering it's Valentine's Day again
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Frank's antisocial tendencies. The 'again' suggests this is a recurring nightmare for him.

jokes.text: Well, who cares, you know? It's just Valentine's Day. It's also her birthday.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to comfort Frank about missing Valentine's Day
jokes.punchline: Frank reveals it's also his wife's birthday, making it much worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation timing and structure. Economy is excellent - three words destroy Liz's attempt at comfort.

jokes.text: Did you just come from a Suzanne Somers look-alike contest?
jokes.setup: Someone's appearance after performing
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to dated celebrity with particular look
jokes.explanation: Solid insult with specific reference. Frank's character voice comes through in the pop culture reference.

jokes.text: Actually, I just came from performing at Vagina Day. Is that an offshoot of 'The Vagina Monologues'? No. We are in no way affiliated with 'The Vagina Monologues.'
jokes.setup: Question about connection to famous feminist play
jokes.punchline: Defensive denial of any affiliation
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist premise with perfect defensive response. Sets up the even funnier explanation to follow.

jokes.text: Vagina Day is a charity event founded by a group of celebrities who have, for whatever reason, never been asked to participate in 'The Vagina Monologues.'
jokes.setup: Explanation of the alternative event
jokes.punchline: It's basically for celebrities rejected from the real thing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment for Jenna's insecurity and need to be included. The premise is both absurd and perfectly believable for her world.

jokes.text: Every February 14th, we improvise monologues about our lady parts for the homeless. Oh, to benefit the homeless? No, just for them.
jokes.setup: Assumption that it's to help homeless people
jokes.punchline: Clarification that it's literally just performed for them, not to help them
jokes.explanation: Masterful distinction that reveals the complete pointlessness and self-serving nature of the event. Perfect economy in the correction.

jokes.text: My vagina is a flower. A weird, ugly flower.
jokes.setup: Example of her vagina monologue performance
jokes.punchline: The qualifier that undermines any poetry or beauty
jokes.explanation: Good example of Jenna's self-deprecating honesty undermining her attempts at artistry. The escalation works well.

jokes.text: Marry, boff, kill? Beyoncé, Paula Abdul, and Oprah. Dude, that's beyond easy. Boff Beyoncé, kill Paula, marry Oprah.
jokes.setup: The marry/boff/kill game with celebrities
jokes.punchline: Frank's matter-of-fact, practical approach to the choices
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Frank's practical mindset. The Oprah choice for marriage shows good reasoning.

jokes.text: Marry, boff, kill... Cerie, Liz, and Jenna. Once again, no-brainer. Marry Liz, get with Cerie, kill Jenna.
jokes.setup: Game continues with people they actually work with
jokes.punchline: Casual decision to 'kill' Jenna, a coworker present in the room
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor from the inappropriate nature of playing this game about coworkers. Sets up Jenna's reaction.

jokes.text: I can hear you!
jokes.setup: The writers casually discussing killing her
jokes.punchline: Jenna's hurt reaction to being present for her own 'murder'
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat that punctures the casual cruelty. Economy is excellent - two words convey hurt and indignation.

jokes.text: But thank you for saying that you would marry me.
jokes.setup: Liz being told she would be the 'marry' choice
jokes.punchline: Her grateful acceptance of this backhanded compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz character moment showing her low self-esteem and gratitude for minimal validation. Great deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: No, I'm not with so many men that it's impossible for me to guess. Well, that is just... Oh. Oh, well, you know what? I found the card, actually. They're from your mom. Yeah. So tell your gay mom I said thanks.
jokes.setup: Liz defensive about her dating life to florist
jokes.punchline: Escalates to insulting the florist's mother out of spite
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Liz's defensiveness leading to petty insults. Character integration is excellent for her insecurity.

jokes.text: No, I'm not with so many men that it's impossible for me to guess.
jokes.setup: Liz calling florist about mystery flowers
jokes.punchline: Defensive response revealing the opposite is true
jokes.explanation: Good Liz defensive moment that reveals her romantic desperation

jokes.text: Marriage is a competition. And after 18 years of overtime, I am finally going to claim victory.
jokes.setup: Jack giving advice about marriage
jokes.punchline: Framing marriage as a competitive sport with victory conditions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack philosophy that reveals his competitive nature and weird relationship dynamics. Very quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Bone Osama bin Laden. To shame him. And then his own people would murder him.
jokes.setup: Playing marry/boff/kill with controversial figures
jokes.punchline: Strategic reasoning for the choice based on geopolitical consequences
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that takes the game to absurd strategic levels. The logic is both disturbing and funny.

jokes.text: I'd marry Toofer 'cause he's classy. I'd kill Lutz. Sorry, Lutz. The thought that you would do anything to me is awesome.
jokes.setup: Cerie playing the game with her male coworkers
jokes.punchline: Lutz's pathetic gratitude for even being considered for murder
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Lutz's desperate need for any attention, even hypothetical violence. Dark and pathetic.

jokes.text: And I'd boff Kenneth. What?! Really?
jokes.setup: Expected Kenneth would be the 'kill' choice
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's shock and excitement at being the 'boff' choice
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that subverts expectations. Kenneth's innocent shock makes it funnier.

jokes.text: 'Cause you're a big phony. What? Everything about you is fake. Your tan's fake. Your hair is fake. Not the front.
jokes.setup: Frank calling out Jenna's fakeness
jokes.punchline: Jenna's defensive correction about which part of her hair is real
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jenna's vanity and need to defend even small details. The specificity is funny.

jokes.text: My vagina is a convenience store... clean and reliable and closed on Christmas.
jokes.setup: Another example of her vagina monologue
jokes.punchline: Comparing vagina to a convenience store with specific business hours
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist metaphor with perfect specific details. The 'closed on Christmas' detail elevates it from good to great.

jokes.text: I mean, if you met her, you might think she's wonderful. But, believe me, she is the succubus from the bowels of hell.
jokes.setup: Jack trying to convince people his ex-wife is terrible
jokes.punchline: Admits she seems wonderful but insists she's literally demonic
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's conflicted feelings. The contrast between 'wonderful' and 'succubus' is effective.

jokes.text: I wish I could touch her boobs again. She really had a fabulous pair of boobs.
jokes.setup: Jack's conflicted feelings about his ex-wife
jokes.punchline: His crude admission of what he misses most
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's shallow and crude side beneath his sophistication.

jokes.text: I admit sometimes I would fantasize about her getting various terminal illnesses, and I would nurse her... to her death.
jokes.setup: Jack admitting to dark fantasies about his ex-wife
jokes.punchline: The pause before 'to her death' reveals the sinister nature of the fantasy
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark character reveal with perfect timing. The pause and delivery make the sinister intent clear.

jokes.text: what are you doing tonight?
jokes.setup: Jack's emotional speech about love and finding someone new
jokes.punchline: Immediately hits on the nearest available person
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and economy. The immediate pivot from philosophical to predatory is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: We have reports of a nitrogen leak on this floor. Oh, no, our room is fine. I mean, we don't smell it. Oh, it's odorless, sir.
jokes.setup: Building inspector checking for gas leak
jokes.punchline: Reveals the 'inspector' knows more than he should, suggesting it's Tracy in disguise
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Tracy's elaborate roleplay scheme. The 'odorless' response reveals the deception cleverly.

jokes.text: I think it's coming... from my butt. Oh, no! I'm married!
jokes.setup: The 'inspector' detecting heat sources
jokes.punchline: Reveals himself through crude humor, other person realizes the deception
jokes.explanation: Good payoff for the roleplay setup. Tracy's inability to stay in character is perfect for his personality.

jokes.text: Role-play, baby! Give it to me, baby!
jokes.setup: Pete thinking Tracy is part of his Valentine's roleplay
jokes.punchline: Pete enthusiastically engaging in sexual roleplay with wrong person
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Pete's earlier roleplay confession. The mistaken identity creates awkward humor.

jokes.text: Happy Valentimes!
jokes.setup: Valentine's Day greeting
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'Valentine's' as 'Valentimes'
jokes.explanation: Simple mispronunciation humor. Not particularly sophisticated but fits character voice.

jokes.text: No. It gives me a headache. This is a Krug Clos du Mesnil, and I was saving it for a special occasion.
jokes.setup: Jack offering expensive champagne
jokes.punchline: Angie's dismissive response to what's clearly a very expensive bottle
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast showing class differences. Jack's wine knowledge vs. Angie's practical concerns.

jokes.text: I clean this, too, or, uh, just the sex?
jokes.setup: Eastern European cleaning lady in awkward situation
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact question about cleaning up after sex
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor from practical question in awkward situation. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: You tell G. E. light-bulb man, I want 'Sophie's Choice' out of my suite!
jokes.setup: Cleaning lady giving Jack a message
jokes.punchline: Refers to someone as 'Sophie's Choice' and calls Jack 'light-bulb man'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character voice with unexpected cultural references. 'Light-bulb man' for GE executive is perfect.

jokes.text: In fact, she's pretty sure I'm the reincarnated soul of Adrian Twyfer. He was our town minister who died in an organ fire.
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining his old soul nature
jokes.punchline: Specific backstory about reincarnation and bizarre death
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth moment with specific absurd backstory. 'Organ fire' is both specific and inexplicable.

jokes.text: No, I think she's kind of like Dr Pepper.
jokes.setup: Question about whether Dr. Laura is a real doctor
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's innocent comparison to Dr Pepper
jokes.explanation: Brilliant innocent observation that perfectly captures Kenneth's worldview. Economy is perfect.

jokes.text: This is the second-worst Valentine's Day we ever had.
jokes.setup: Terrible Valentine's Day situation
jokes.punchline: Implies there was an even worse one
jokes.explanation: Good understatement that suggests even worse past experiences. Economy works well.

jokes.text: Marry, boff, kill... Bianca. Which do you want to do? All of them.
jokes.setup: Liz applying the game to Jack's relationship
jokes.punchline: Jack's honest admission of his confused feelings
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that crystallizes Jack's entire relationship dysfunction. Two words reveal everything about his psychology.

jokes.text: You wear your thrift-store T-shirts and your big, weird glasses and everybody says, 'Oh, look at Frank. He's so cool. He has a hat that says Extra Cheese.'
jokes.setup: Jenna calling out Frank's affected coolness
jokes.punchline: Specific example of his inexplicable hipster accessories
jokes.explanation: Good character insight about performative coolness. The 'Extra Cheese' hat is a perfect specific detail.

jokes.text: Okay, fine. I pooted. It's 3:00 in the morning. Are you happy?
jokes.setup: Jenna finally doing something real and human
jokes.punchline: Her childish word 'pooted' and defensive attitude
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jenna's vulnerability. 'Pooted' is perfect for her vanity about bodily functions.

jokes.text: If I blew it, then how did I get her underpants?
jokes.setup: Kenneth being mocked for blowing it with Cerie
jokes.punchline: His innocent proof that suggests something happened
jokes.explanation: Perfect innocent Kenneth moment that suggests more than it reveals. Good misdirection about what actually happened.

jokes.text: I wouldn't be surprised if that 5-inch scar across my abdomen was suddenly gone.
jokes.setup: Jack saying it's like the marriage never happened
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to serious physical trauma
jokes.explanation: Dark hint at the violent nature of Jack and Bianca's relationship, delivered casually. Raises disturbing questions.

jokes.text: I'm gonna shut it down, leave it vacant, open the windows, and let nature have at it.
jokes.setup: Jack's petty revenge plan for the Arby's
jokes.punchline: Elaborate plan to destroy perfectly good restaurant out of spite
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Jack's pettiness. The specific plan to 'let nature have at it' is beautifully vindictive.

jokes.text: You know, I've heard that, in Greece, they have to change Charlton Heston's name on movie posters to Charlton Easton because in Greek the word 'heston' means 'to poop yourself.'
jokes.setup: Making conversation about Planet of the Apes poster
jokes.punchline: Elaborate fake trivia about Charlton Heston's name in Greece
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific fake trivia that sounds almost believable. The elaborate setup for a poop joke is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: I prefer 'law stylist.'
jokes.setup: Being called a lawyer
jokes.punchline: Pretentious alternative job title
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing millennial workplace pretensions. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: Happy Valentimes!
jokes.setup: Ending the episode
jokes.punchline: Callback to the earlier mispronunciation
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to bookend the episode. Not particularly strong but provides closure.

tmdb_overview: It's Valentine's Day, and the TGS crew is celebrating by pulling an all nighter to put together a new episode. While Pete completely forgets the holiday, Kenneth and Cerie may be starting an office romance. Meanwhile, Liz wonders if Dennis is back in her life after she receives flowers from a secret admirer and newly divorced Jack wants to party with Tracy, who for once wants to spend a night alone with his wife instead.
insight: Season finale escalates absurdist chaos to 85.5, sustaining 2.59 jokes per minute.
